name: Infer
comment:
This is the module implementing type inference. You'll also need at least `Infer.Expression`.
@docs typeOf

values.name: typeOf
values.comment:
Returns a computation that yields the type of the input expression
with the specified environment.

name: Infer.Scheme

aliases.name: Environment
aliases.comment: Holds all names defined in outer scopes.

aliases.name: Scheme
aliases.comment:
A type scheme represents a variable definition, for example a named function.
When the variable is used, the scheme must be converted into a concrete type.
The listed type variables are the ones that the type is generic over. It may
contain others that represent for example types of things defined higher up.

values.name: freeVariables
values.comment: Variables that are not bound by the type scheme.

values.name: freshTypevar
values.comment: Generates a type variable with the identifier one greater than the last.

values.name: generalize
values.comment:
Converts a type into a type scheme that is generic over all the type variables
in the type not coming from the environment.

values.name: instantiate
values.comment:
Converts a scheme into a concrete type by swapping the generic type
variables for fresh ones.

values.name: substitute
values.comment: Applies a substitution on a type scheme without touching the generic type vars

unions.name: Expression
unions.comment: Translate your expressions to this type in order to be able to perform type inference on them.

name: Infer.Monad

aliases.name: Monad
aliases.comment: Represents a stateful computation that can fail.

values.name: andMap
values.comment:
map over one more value. Arguments are reversed in order to work
well with `<|`.
values.type: Monad y -> Monad (y -> z) -> Monad z

values.name: andThen
values.comment: `andThen` for this particular monad.
values.type: (a -> Monad b) -> Monad a -> Monad b

values.name: combine
values.comment: Lifts the monads out of a list.

values.name: finalValue
values.comment: Computes the value of a computation.
values.type: Int -> Monad a -> Result String a

values.name: fromResult
values.comment: Un-specialize a Result.

values.name: map
values.comment: `map` for this particular monad.

values.name: map2
values.comment: `map2` for this particular monad.
values.type: (a -> b -> c) -> Monad a -> Monad b -> Monad c

values.name: map3
values.comment: `map3` for this particular monad.
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d) -> Monad a -> Monad b -> Monad c -> Monad d

values.name: map4
values.comment: `map4` for this particular monad.
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> e) -> Monad a -> Monad b -> Monad c -> Monad d -> Monad e

values.name: pure
values.comment: Put a value into the monad. Will not advance the fresh name supply nor cause an error.

name: Infer.Type

unions.name: Type
unions.comment: Represents Elm types. TAny is for type variables.

aliases.name: Substitution
aliases.comment: Tells what values type variables get.

values.name: bool
values.comment: Bool

values.name: char
values.comment: Char

values.name: float
values.comment: Float

values.name: int
values.comment: Int

values.name: string
values.comment: String

values.name: substitute
values.comment: Swap out type variables according to substitution

values.name: toString
values.comment: Textual representation of a type

values.name: unify
values.comment:
Returns the substitutions necessary to transform either type
  into their lowest common denominator.
  Returns an error if not possible.

values.name: union
values.comment: Returns a type that conforms to both supplied types

values.name: variables
values.comment: Returns the (free) type variables of the type.
